summary: Matched two mnname functions, `fn_80238540` and `mnName_80239FFC`, with clean build verification and 100.0 fuzzy match for all changed functions. The work replaced placeholders with typed menu input/cleanup logic, added a local `MnName_GObj` overlay, and updated related headers so HSD_GObj/HSD_JObj/HSD_Text usage is explicit.

key_files.reason: Implemented `fn_80238540` and `mnName_80239FFC`; added includes for `mnmain.h`, `mnnamenew.h`, and baselib GObj support.
Added `MnName_GObj` struct and converted several `UNK_PARAMS` declarations to typed `HSD_GObj*` signatures.
Added baselib forward include and changed `mnNameNew_MainInput` to take `HSD_GObj*`.

what_changed: `fn_80238540` was decompiled as an `HSD_GObj*` input dispatcher switching on `(s32) mn_804A04F0.x10`.
Dispatcher cases call `mnName_MainInput(gobj)`, `mnNameNew_MainInput(gobj)`, or `mnName_ConfirmNameDeleteInput(gobj)` for states 0, 1, and 2 respectively.

`mnName_80239FFC` was decompiled as a cleanup routine that casts the object to `MnName_GObj`, extracts a JObj, removes its child tree with `HSD_JObjRemoveAll`, frees `mn->text` with `HSD_SisLib_803A5CC4`, and nulls the text pointer.
Added `typedef struct MnName_GObj` with `HSD_GObj gobj` at +0x00, unknown `x38`, and `HSD_Text* text` at +0x3C.

Updated prototypes for `mnName_ConfirmNameDeleteInput`, `mnName_MainInput`, `fn_80238540`, `mnName_80239FFC`, and `mnNameNew_MainInput` away from generic `UNK_PARAMS` where evidence supported `HSD_GObj*`.
smart_moves: Typed the cross-module input path with `HSD_GObj*`, making the dispatcher and called input handlers line up cleanly.
Introduced a named struct overlay for the extended GObj data instead of leaving all access as raw offsets.
Preserved matching-sensitive control flow in `fn_80238540`: an integer cast on the global state, direct switch cases, and no visible default case.
Used explicit null handling in the cleanup path before calling `HSD_JObjRemoveAll` and before freeing the SIS text object.
Responded to review direction by moving away from byte-pointer arithmetic and toward actual GObj/JObj struct field access.
decomp_lessons: When an input dispatcher is blocked, first type the neighboring handlers; replacing `UNK_PARAMS` with `HSD_GObj*` can expose the simple switch/call structure.
For GObj-backed menu objects, define small local wrapper structs with offset comments when only a few fields are known.
Avoid pointer math against `HSD_GObj` when existing baselib structs or local overlays can describe the access.
Cleanup/destructor-like menu routines may require exact ordering: derive child JObj, remove it, then conditionally free text and clear the field.
Cross-header typing matters: `mnname.c` needed declarations from `mnnamenew.h`, and `mnnamenew.h` needed baselib forward declarations once `HSD_GObj*` appeared in its prototype.
naming_conventions: Unnamed functions continue to use address-style `fn_80238540` naming.
Known menu name functions use the `mnName_` prefix.
Struct wrapper name `MnName_GObj` follows subsystem plus wrapped baselib type.
Unknown struct fields remain generic, e.g. `x38`, with offset comments documenting layout evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: decomp.dev reported 2 new matches in `main/melee/mn/mnname`: `fn_80238540` at +96 bytes and `mnName_80239FFC` at +92 bytes, +188 bytes total matched code.
The matching dispatcher uses `switch ((s32) mn_804A04F0.x10)` with cases 0, 1, and 2.
`mnName_80239FFC` uses an explicit `if (jobj == NULL) child = NULL; else child = jobj->child;` shape before `HSD_JObjRemoveAll(child)`.
The final diff accesses JObj child data through `HSD_JObj*` rather than the reviewed raw `u8*` plus offset pattern.

review_feedback.feedback: For `mnName_80239FFC`, use the actual `jobj` struct, not pointer math.
review_feedback.evidence: Review hunk objected to `u8* p = (u8*) gobj;` and `jobj = *(HSD_JObj**) (p + 0x30);`; final diff uses `MnName_GObj*`, `HSD_JObj*`, and `jobj->child`.

follow_up_queries: Find remaining `mnname.h` or `mnnamenew.h` prototypes still using `UNK_PARAMS` that likely take `HSD_GObj*`.
Search for other menu cleanup functions that pair `HSD_JObjRemoveAll` with `HSD_SisLib_803A5CC4`.
Look for GObj extension overlays similar to `MnName_GObj` to standardize field naming around +0x38 and +0x3C.
Compare `fn_80238540` with other menu state dispatchers using a global state field and switch cases.
Audit any remaining raw pointer math against `HSD_GObj` in mn/menu code and replace it with struct-backed access where evidence allows.
